disease.name: IgA Nephropathy (Berger's Disease)
disease.note: Most common glomerulonephritis worldwide. IgA deposits in mesangium. Classic presentation: hematuria coinciding with mucosal infections. Variable prognosis.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: MESAN (MEST-C) score: M (mesangial), E (endocapillary), S (segmental), A (tubular atrophy), N (crescents). M1, E1, S1, T1-2, C1-2 indicate worse prognosis.

subtypes.name: IgA Nephropathy - Low Risk
subtypes.management: ACEi/ARB for BP and proteinuria. Lifestyle modifications (low salt, exercise). Monitor BP, creatinine, urine ACR q3-6 months. No immunosuppression needed. Fish oil may slow progression (controversial).

subtypes.name: IgA Nephropathy - High Risk
subtypes.management: Maximal supportive care (ACEi/ARB, SGLT2i, BP control, low salt). After 3 months, if proteinuria >1 g/day: consider corticosteroids (prednisolone 0.5 mg/kg alt days x 6 months) or targeted-release budesonide (TRF-budesonide). Mycophenolate mofetil if steroid contraindicated. SGLT2i (dapagliflozin). Consider tonsillectomy (controversial, reduces mucosal IgA triggers).

subtypes.name: IgA Vasculitis (Henoch-Schönlein Purpura)
subtypes.management: Supportive care for mild cases. NSAIDs for arthritis. Corticosteroids for severe abdominal pain or GI bleeding. Renal involvement: treat like IgA nephropathy based on proteinuria. Severe crescentic disease: pulse steroids + cyclophosphamide. Usually self-limiting in children.

laboratory.name: Complement C3, C4
laboratory.indication: normal in IgA (vs low in PSGN/lupus)

imaging.name: Renal biopsy
imaging.indication: IgA deposition in mesangium on immunofluorescence, MEST-C score

pathways.criteria: proteinuria <0.5 g/day, normal GFR, normal BP
pathways.treatment: ACEi/ARB if proteinuria >0.3 g/day. Optimize BP <130/80. Annual monitoring. Lifestyle measures.

pathways.criteria: proteinuria 0.5-1 g/day despite 3 months of ACEi/ARB
pathways.treatment: Maximize ACEi/ARB. Add SGLT2i (dapagliflozin). BP target <120/80. Consider TRF-budesonide 16 mg/day if proteinuria >1 g/day. Avoid prolonged steroids if possible.

pathways.criteria: proteinuria >1 g/day despite 3 months maximal supportive care, declining GFR
pathways.treatment: Corticosteroids (prednisolone 0.5 mg/kg alt days x 6 months) OR TRF-budesonide. MMF if steroid failure/contraindication. Consider tonsillectomy. For RPGN: pulse steroids + cyclophosphamide.
